title: Navigating the High Cost of AI Compute
body: Source: Industry Analysis on AI Infrastructure Economics
Focus: Compute costs and infrastructure decisions for AI companies

title: Agenda
body: Compute supply-demand imbalance in generative AI
Capital allocation patterns in AI startups
GPT-3 training costs and infrastructure requirements
GPU instance pricing variations across vendors
Cloud versus data center infrastructure decisions
Training dataset scale and web data consumption

title: Severe Compute Supply Shortage
body: Generative AI faces compute demand exceeding supply by approximately 10 times.
This supply constraint has made compute access a critical competitive factor.
The imbalance affects both training and inference operations across the industry.

title: Compute Dominates Startup Spending
body: Many AI startups spending over 80% of capital on compute.
Access to compute at the lowest total cost has become a determining factor for AI companies.
Infrastructure costs represent the primary operational expense for generative AI firms.

title: GPT-3 Model Scale
body: GPT-3 has 175 billion parameters.
Training requires over one terabyte of memory.
This scale represents the benchmark for large language model infrastructure requirements.

title: GPT-3 Training Cost Range
body: GPT-3 costs between $500,000 and $4.6 million per training run on NVIDIA A100 GPUs.
The wide cost range reflects differences in vendor pricing and infrastructure efficiency.
These figures apply specifically to training runs using A100 GPU infrastructure.

title: A100 GPU Instance Pricing Baseline
body: A100 instance pricing ranges from roughly $0.25 to $1 per hour.
Nearly 4x differences exist among vendors for equivalent compute resources.
Pricing variations significantly impact total training and inference costs.

title: Premium Cloud Provider Pricing
body: AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud charge premium rates for GPU instances.
These major hyperscalers position at the higher end of the pricing spectrum.
Premium pricing reflects broader service ecosystems and global infrastructure availability.

title: Lower-Cost GPU Vendors
body: Coreweave and Lambda Labs offer lower costs compared to major cloud providers.
These specialized vendors focus on GPU compute for AI workloads.
Cost advantages can reach nearly 4x versus premium providers for A100 instances.

title: Cloud versus Data Center Decision Threshold
body: Startups should use cloud rather than build data centers unless infrastructure spending exceeds $50 million annually.
Below this threshold, cloud economics remain favorable despite premium pricing.
Capital efficiency and operational flexibility favor cloud for most AI startups.

title: Training Data Scale
body: Training datasets already use approximately 10% of all web pages via Common Crawl.
This represents the current scope of web data being leveraged for model training.
Common Crawl serves as the primary source for large-scale web-based training datasets.

title: Strategic Implications
body: Compute access and cost optimization have become determining competitive factors.
Infrastructure decisions between cloud and owned data centers require $50 million annual spend threshold analysis.
Vendor selection can yield 4x cost differences on identical GPU infrastructure.
Data availability approaching limits with 10% of web pages already in training datasets.
